reasoning: Well-constructed padding oracle challenge with a genuine twist: the code implements a timing-based side channel (60ms delay on valid padding) with unified error responses, yet the description incorrectly claims two distinct error codes — the actual implementation is harder than advertised. ISO 7816-4 padding instead of PKCS#7, XOR-masked CRC32 checksums, and artificial jitter add meaningful layers, though the core attack remains a standard padding oracle once the timing oracle is identified. Excellent flag isolation with build-time encryption and file removal.
